question: The measured <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>BOD</mi></math> values tor four difterent water samples <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> are as tollows: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn></math> ppm; <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>18</mn></math> ppm; <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>21</mn></math> ppm; <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn></math> ppm. The water samples which can be called as highly polluted with organic wastes, are
solution:
The amount of oxygen required by bacteria to break down the organic matter present in a certain volume of a sample of water, is called Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD).

The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically.

Clean water would have BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas highly polluted water could have a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

Hence,Among the given samples, sample number B and C are polluted.

q: The role of depressants in 'Froth Floation method' is to
question: The role of depressants in 'Froth Floation method' is to
options: selectively prevent one component of the ore from coming to the froth.
reduce the consumption of oil for froth formation.
enhance non-wettability of the mineral particles.
solution:
In the froth floatation process, the role of the depressants is to separate two sulphide ores by selectively preventing one ore from forming froth.

For example, to separate two sulphide ores, PbS and ZnS,NaCN is utilised. NaCN forms a combination with zinc on the surface of zinc sulphide, Na2ZnCN4, preventing ZnS from foaming selectively.

4NaCN + ZnS⇒Na2ZnCN4+Na2S

question: The number of radial and angular nodes in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></math> orbital are, respectively
solution:
Number of Radial nodes =n-l-1

Number of angular nodes =l

Where,

l=Azimuthal quantum number n=principal quantum number 

For 4d orbital, n=4 and l=2

In 4d orbital the number of Radial nodes=4-2-1=1

In 4d orbital the number of Angular nodes =l=2.

Thus, the number of radial and angular nodes in 4d orbital are 1 and 2 respectively.

solution:
Prime factorisation of 36=2×2×3×3

So, for number having G.C.D with 36 to be 2, the number should be odd multiple of 2 and does not have factor 3 and 9

⇒Odd multiple of 2 are

102,106,110,114…….998  225 numbers

⇒No. of multiples of 3 are

102,114,126…….990 75 numbers

Which also include multiple of 9

Hence, Required =225-75=150

q: If the probability that a randomly chosen <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math>-digit number formed by using digits <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn></math> only is a multiple of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>21</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>96</mn><mi>p</mi></math> is equal to _____.
question: If the probability that a randomly chosen <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math>-digit number formed by using digits <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn></math> only is a multiple of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>21</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>96</mn><mi>p</mi></math> is equal to _____.
solution:
We have to choose 6 digit number by using 1 & 8 and it must be divisible by 21,

So, equal number of digits 1 and 8 should be used to form a number divisible by 3 and 7. (Or all the digits are either 1 or 8)

In all cases whatever number is formed using six 1' or six 8's or three 1's + three 8's, that number can be splited into sum of multiples of 1001,1008,8001,8008 (all of them are divisible by 7). So, all the numbers thus formed will be divisible by 21,

Example: 811188=8001×102+1008×10+1008

Number of such numbers =6!3!3!+2=22

Required Probability, p=2226=1132

∴ 96p=1122×96=33

Given below are two statements: One is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A: If we move from poles to equator, the direction of acceleration due to gravity of earth always points towards the center of earth without any variation in its magnitude.

Reason R: At equator, the direction of acceleration due to the gravity is towards the center of earth.

In the light of above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements: One is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A: If we move from poles to equator, the direction of acceleration due to gravity of earth always points towards the center of earth without any variation in its magnitude.

Reason R: At equator, the direction of acceleration due to the gravity is towards the center of earth.

In the light of above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
options: Assertion and reason both are correct and reason is correct explanation of assertion.
Assertion and reason both are correct but reason is not correct explanation of assertion.
Assertion is true but reason is false
Assertion is false but reason is true
solution:
Value of effective g increases as we move from the equator to pole because on the equator its value is less due to earth's rotational motion and consequent centrifugal force. 
Moreover, the equator of the earth is at a larger distance from the centre of the earth as compared to the poles. This is another reason why g is greater on the pole than the equator.

At the equator, direction of gravitational pull and centrifugal force are in same line, hence net force is towards the centre of the Earth.

As argon is monoatomic gas, so it has three degree of freedom f and oxygen is a diatomic gas, so its degree of freedom is five.

We know that average kinetic energy per molecule is given by KE=f2kT, where, k is Boltzmann's constant and T is temperature.

Here, T=27°C is fixed for both gas.

So, KEArKEO=32kT52kT=35
